task_completion.description: Create at least three new partition walls to define distinct zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D floor plan pane in Sweet Home 3D. There should be at least three new interior walls drawn to divide the open space into distinct rooms or zones.

task_completion.description: Add at least four text labels to the floor plan identifying functional zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D floor plan pane. There should be at least four text labels (e.g., 'Main Shooting Bay', 'Changing Room', 'Equipment Storage') placed in different areas.

task_completion.description: Furnish the studio with appropriate items including desks/tables, chairs, storage shelves, and lighting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D floor plan and 3D view panes. The space should be heavily furnished (expecting around 35 items total) including desks or tables, chairs/sofas for a lounge, storage shelves, and lighting equipment.

task_completion.description: Generate a 3D photo rendering of the completed studio and save it to ~/Desktop/studio_3d_photo.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Sweet Home 3D 'Create photo' dialog being used, and a file named `studio_3d_photo.png` visible on the Desktop or in a file manager.

task_completion.description: Save the modified Sweet Home 3D plan file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the agent clicking 'Save' in Sweet Home 3D, or the absence of an unsaved changes asterisk in the window title after modifications have been made.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Sweet Home 3D GUI to build the studio.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sweet Home 3D application open with the agent actively using the mouse and menus to draw walls, drag furniture, and add text.

integrity.description: The 3D photo rendering was generated using Sweet Home 3D's built-in tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Create photo' rendering dialog in Sweet Home 3D with a progress bar or the final rendered image inside the application before saving.

integrity.description: The agent built the studio progressively rather than loading a pre-made file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the gradual addition of walls, labels, and furniture to the empty shell, rather than a fully completed studio appearing instantly in one step.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The verifier strictly scores the agent on generating at least 3 new walls (20 pts), 4 text labels (25 pts), and 35 total pieces of furniture including specific counts for desks, chairs, shelves, lamps, sofas, and decor (20 pts). It also checks for a 3D photo rendered to ~/Desktop/studio_3d_photo.png (20 pts) and the saved modified plan file (15 pts). Pass threshold is 70/100.
